business_ideas.idea: Digital AI clone trained on personal content corpus to advise founders
Plaid for AI tools—portable memory/context layer across AI apps
Specialized AI advisors for sales compensation planning, SDR scripts
AI voice support/customer service startups targeting boring markets
AI for VC pitch deck review and board meeting prep
Restaurant tech disruption via physical-world logistics (Owner)
AI legal startups targeting sleepy markets

predictions.text: Apps die; chat becomes 24/7 interface. CRMs become invisible plumbing.
Every founder will have AI content double. Tools change yearly but practice permanent.
Big companies won't leverage AI better; edges will disrupt from zero.
OpenAI will be worth $1 trillion. Wealth gaps widen; 100+ billionaires in SaaS alone.
Kids today won't use software interfaces. They'll chat. Interface era ending.

disagreements: Jason argues only #1 place in market matters; Shaan counters: small teams can hit 'one-hit wonders' with $8M ARR cash flows without being #1. Jason concedes lifestyle businesses are dead due to competition but admits base hits now fund entire portfolios.
dynamics: Jason has shifted from grumpy cynic to optimistic builder. Upbeat energy; contrasts old Jason (skeptical) with new Jason (all-in on AI). Sam and Shaan push back on nuance; banter reveals Jason's intensity and founder obsession with market dominance. Jason uncomfortable on camera but strong in conversation.
frameworks: Zero→1M (now easy in AI); 1→10M (unlikely); 10→100M (inevitable) disruption curve
MCP as AI-API protocol layer (language for apps to speak to each other)
Memory moat in AI (like Google/Yahoo ecosystem lock)
Relentless recruiting: find top 200, talk to all, not 5 fake interviews
Cold email as founder signal (how good is idea + founder in one message?)
Founder intensity/desperation (take market #1 or die trying)
learnings: Users confess fears to AI that they hide from humans—AI functions as therapy
Specialized AI (20M words narrowcast) beats generic ChatGPT on domain tasks
Memory in LLMs is a durable moat but can be replicated in 1 month of training
Content compounds slowly but predictably when done daily for years
Relentless recruiting (2+ candidates/day) is force multiplier; 90% of leaders lack this
Non-tech founders (events, media) now do $25M/5-person via AI replacing teams
Hallucinations fixable by 10-15 min daily training; consistency improves quickly
Good founders solve via email; bad founders need warm intros and meetings
Interfaces are dying faster than incumbents can defend; B2B apps already dated in 2025

banger_quotes.quote: Now zero to one is very likely in AI. You build an incredible tool, do million in week.
The interface is dying. You won't log into CRM. You'll ask ChatGPT for your deals.
Relentless recruiters find 200 best people and actually talk to all of them, not five.
Digital stunt double; intellectual version of you in every meeting without paying you.
I used to think products were durable. Now I don't think any are. That changes everything.
Small teams can ride wave, take cash, fund five more doors. One-hit wonders OK now.

notable: Jason's AI trained on 20M words of his own writing is 90% better at founder advising than him. Companies replacing entire content + design teams (ghostwriters, reviewers) with single person + Claude/ChatGPT. Shift to pull-market dynamics (Owner: $1M/week, unprecedented pull).
